title: pouteria rhynchocarpa
intro: Pouteria rhynchocarpa, also known as the 'Makamaka' or 'Pouteria', is a tropical tree species native to the Pacific Islands and Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Polynesian medicine, where its bark and roots are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Pouteria rhynchocarpa include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of wounds, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Pouteria rhynchocarpa is traditionally prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its herbal preparations are also used in modern medicine as an ingredient in topical creams, ointments, and other pharmaceutical products.
